search_terms_used: lactate threshold endurance performance prediction field test validity systematic review meta-analysis pubmed
MLSS maximal lactate steady state field test determination running cycling validity reliability pubmed
LT1 LT2 first second lactate threshold ventilatory threshold aerobic anaerobic distinction endurance training pubmed
Jamnick 2018 2020 lactate threshold manipulated variables determination exercise intensity pubmed
Heck 1985 onset blood lactate accumulation 4 mmol fixed threshold exercise pubmed
Kindermann Simon Keul 1979 anaerobic threshold significance blood lactate workload pubmed
coverage_notes: Strong coverage: foundational threshold concepts (Kindermann 1979 aerobic-anaerobic transition, Heck 1985 4mmol justification), LT-performance relationships (Yoshida 1987, Faude 2009 comprehensive validity review), measurement methods (Svedahl & MacIntosh 2003, Goodwin 2007 clinician guide), MLSS reliability (Hauser 2013), predictive value comparison (Heuberger 2018), protocol effects on validity (Jamnick 2018), and modern critique of exercise intensity methods (Jamnick 2020). Covers evolution from fixed thresholds to individualized methods.

papers.title: The significance of the aerobic-anaerobic transition for the determination of work load intensities during endurance training
papers.journal: European Journal of Applied Physiology and Occupational Physiology
papers.notes: Examined aerobic and anaerobic thresholds (4 mmol/L lactate) in cross-country skiers to determine workload intensities for endurance training prescription.

papers.title: Justification of the 4-mmol/l lactate threshold
papers.notes: Provided the scientific justification for using a fixed 4 mmol/L blood lactate concentration as the onset of blood lactate accumulation (OBLA) threshold.

papers.title: Blood lactate parameters related to aerobic capacity and endurance performance
papers.journal: European Journal of Applied Physiology and Occupational Physiology
papers.notes: Compared four blood lactate threshold descriptors (LT, LT1, LT2, OBLA) against aerobic capacity and 12-minute running performance in 19 untrained females.

papers.title: Anaerobic threshold: the concept and methods of measurement
papers.notes: Reviewed the anaerobic threshold concept and measurement methods including MLSS, lactate minimum test, LT, OBLA, individual anaerobic threshold, and ventilatory threshold.

papers.title: Blood lactate measurements and analysis during exercise: a guide for clinicians
papers.journal: Journal of Diabetes Science and Technology
papers.notes: Reviewed practical blood lactate measurement and analysis methods during exercise for clinical and athletic performance testing applications.

papers.title: Lactate threshold concepts: how valid are they?
papers.notes: Critically reviewed 25 different lactate threshold concepts evaluating their physiological validity, repeatability, and practical application for performance prediction.

papers.title: Reliability of maximal lactate-steady-state
papers.notes: Examined the test-retest reliability of MLSS determination on cycle ergometry in 32 male subjects, finding 3% coefficient of variability for power output.

papers.title: Repeatability and predictive value of lactate threshold concepts in endurance sports
papers.notes: Compared repeatability and predictive value of multiple lactate threshold concepts for endurance performance, finding Dmax-mod most valid for MLSS estimation.

papers.title: Manipulating graded exercise test variables affects the validity of the lactate threshold and VO2peak
papers.notes: Examined how varying GXT stage length (1-10 min) affects 14 different lactate threshold calculations, finding only 11 of 58 threshold values validly estimated MLSS.

papers.title: An Examination and Critique of Current Methods to Determine Exercise Intensity
papers.notes: Critically examined current methods for determining and prescribing exercise intensity, noting lack of agreed framework for assessing construct validity of threshold concepts.
